suite: COMBINED CLOSEOUT R2 — Phase E FIX E5: fail-closed result-artifact freshness static/functional self-test

results.test: generateRunId() returns a non-empty string
generateRunId() returns a fresh value on each call (never reused)
computeSourceHash() is deterministic for identical inputs
computeSourceHash() changes when file content changes
computeSourceHash() is sensitive to input order
writeResultAtomic() writes readable JSON on first write
writeResultAtomic() fully replaces prior content on a second write (no merge/partial overwrite)
writeResultAtomic() leaves no leftover temp file in the target directory
buildRuntimeCrashRow() row is well-formed {test, result, evidence}
buildRuntimeCrashRow() evidence contains only the bounded errorName, never the full message/stack
buildRuntimeCrashRow() falls back to UnknownError when the thrown value has no .name
validateResultFreshness() ACCEPTS a current, well-formed result matching the expected sourceHash
validateResultFreshness() REJECTS a stale result claiming decision=PASS while completed is not true
validateResultFreshness() REJECTS a result whose sourceHash does not match the current sources
results.evidence: {"valid":false,"reasons":["sourceHash does not match the current sources"]}

results.test: validateResultFreshness() REJECTS a result with completed !== true
validateResultFreshness() REJECTS a result with an empty runId
validateResultFreshness() REJECTS a result with a missing runId field entirely
validateResultFreshness() REJECTS a result containing a malformed row (missing test/result)
results.evidence: {"valid":false,"reasons":["one or more result rows are malformed"]}

results.test: validateResultFreshness() REJECTS a result with an empty results array
validateResultFreshness() REJECTS a result with neither generatedAt nor completedAt
validateResultFreshness() ACCEPTS completedAt as a valid substitute for generatedAt
validateResultFreshness() never throws on a non-object input (fails closed instead)
writeBrowserUnavailableResult() overwrites a stale PASS file (never leaves the old 56/56 PASS content standing)
writeBrowserUnavailableResult() output has completed=true (the suite itself completed its honest environment check)
writeBrowserUnavailableResult() output carries a fresh runId distinct from the stale file's prior runId
writeBrowserUnavailableResult() decision is never PASS for any status value passed in
The environment result written by writeBrowserUnavailableResult() itself passes validateResultFreshness()
buildRunIdentity() preserves completed:true and honest null browser fields when none were launched
buildRunIdentity() defaults every unset field to null/false rather than undefined

epic-2e-j-phase-c-live-app-test.mjs: runId/startedAt/sourceHash are hoisted to MODULE scope (visible to the outer main().catch() handler)
epic-2e-j-phase-c-live-app-test.mjs: calls writeBrowserUnavailableResult() from at least both early-exit branches (Playwright-package-unavailable and Browser-binary-unavailable)
epic-2e-j-phase-c-live-app-test.mjs: success-path output object includes runId/startedAt/completedAt/completed:true/sourceHash
epic-2e-j-phase-c-live-app-test.mjs: writes its official result via writeResultAtomic(), not a bare writeFile()
epic-2e-j-phase-c-live-app-test.mjs: the top-level main().catch() handler itself writes a current crash result before exiting
epic-2e-j-phase-c-live-app-test.mjs: the crash handler logs only err.name, never the full error object

R3-1: computeObservationSmokeDecision() (real imported fn) REJECTS a raw Boolean `false` Result value — never NOT_TESTED, never PASS
R3-1: computeObservationSmokeDecision() (real imported fn) REJECTS a raw Boolean `true` Result value the same way (never silently accepted as PASS)
R3-2: recordCondition() maps condition===true to PASS and EVERY other value (including false) to FAIL — never NOT_TESTED
R3-2: no un-migrated inline Boolean-ternary result assignment remains outside the strict recordStatus/recordCondition API
R3-3: computeObservationSmokeDecision() (real imported fn) REJECTS 22 unexpected NOT_TESTED rows (the exact historical defect count)
R3-4: a single false/FAIL condition anywhere in the Result set prevents Decision PASS (see R3-1 Boolean-false and the one-FAIL case in Part 5 above)
R3-5: computeObservationSmokeDecision() (real imported fn) REJECTS a malformed row (blank test name)
R3-5: computeObservationSmokeDecision() (real imported fn) REJECTS a malformed row (whitespace-only test name)
R3-5: computeObservationSmokeDecision() (real imported fn) REJECTS a malformed row (unrecognized status string)
R3-5: computeObservationSmokeDecision() (real imported fn) REJECTS a malformed row (missing evidence key)
R3-6: installCookieSetterCountingWrapper() (real imported fn) preserves the ORIGINAL getter by exact reference (getterPreserved)
R3-7: evaluateCookiePatchSuccess() (real imported fn) correctly detects a CHANGED setter (setterChanged) — the correctly-oriented check (not the R3 root-cause-A1 inverted check)
R3-7: evaluateCookiePatchSuccess() reports setterPatched=true only when getter preserved AND setter changed AND both are Functions AND configurable/enumerable preserved
R3-7: evaluateCookiePatchSuccess() (real imported fn) correctly reports setterPatched=false when the getter was ALSO changed (proves the ROOT CAUSE A1 inversion bug is fixed, not reintroduced)
R3-6: the installed descriptor is a genuine get/set accessor pair (never a corrupted {value:undefined} data property from a {getter,setter}-keyed object)
R3-8 setup: ensureCookieCompatibility() (real imported fn) installs the Test-only compatibility cookie when native access throws
R3-8: restoreCookieInstrumentation() (real imported fn) restores the EXACT pre-wrapping descriptor by strict Function reference (get/set/configurable/enumerable all identical)
R3-8: the counting wrapper genuinely counted exactly one setter invocation before restoration
R3-9: verifyCompatibilityCleanup() (real imported fn) confirms the __opaqueOriginCookieInstalled marker is fully removed (not merely set to false)
R3-9: verifyCompatibilityCleanup() confirms the compatibility own-property descriptor itself is removed
R3-9: verifyCompatibilityCleanup() confirms the final shape matches the PRISTINE pre-any-installation shape (never compared against the temporary compatibility descriptor)
R3-10: qa/helpers/playwright-opaque-origin-cookie.mjs exports runRealBrowserCookieSelfTest() — a real Chromium/about:blank runtime self-test (FIX A4), not only fake-Document tests
R3-10: the real-Browser Cookie self-test proves a second Page begins clean
R3-10: the real-Browser Cookie self-test verifies no Cookie value is written into its own Result JSON
R3-10: the cookie helper is directly runnable as a standalone script (node qa/helpers/playwright-opaque-origin-cookie.mjs) and writes its own current result file
R3-11: epic-2e-j-phase-c-live-app-test.mjs logs "Live App Final Decision" (FIX C1)
R3-11: epic-2e-j-phase-c-live-app-test.mjs no longer contains the incorrect "Step 7A Final Decision" label
R3-12: qa/baselines/lufa42-production-lock-manifest.json is present and parses as JSON (checked-in, portable — never a machine-specific sibling directory)
R3-12: Production-lock manifest declares a non-empty locked-file set
R3-12: every LOCKED core/ui/index.html file is byte-for-byte identical to the checked-in Production-lock manifest (70 files, SHA-256)

disclaimer: This suite proves the Phase E toolkit (generateRunId, computeSourceHash, writeResultAtomic, buildRunIdentity, buildRuntimeCrashRow, validateResultFreshness, writeBrowserUnavailableResult) via real functional calls in a throwaway temp directory, plus source-text audits proving all four real Browser suites are actually wired to use it. It does not launch a Browser and does not prove real end-to-end crash-handler behavior under an actual Playwright runtime exception — that remains the job of the real Browser suites themselves (which, in this sandbox, can only be exercised along the Browser-unavailable path, confirmed separately by actually running each suite).
